text.text: We stopped by Matcha Cafe Maiko after dinner and shared a waffle cone. It was huge and delicious! Seen it but had not stopped in. Glad we finally did! The soft serve was so good!  We're definitely going back to try more! Such a cute vibe!

originalText.text: We stopped by Matcha Cafe Maiko after dinner and shared a waffle cone. It was huge and delicious! Seen it but had not stopped in. Glad we finally did! The soft serve was so good!  We're definitely going back to try more! Such a cute vibe!

text.text:
Came here after the movies for a quick dessert and a friend said the matcha quality is good here. They are located in a plaza with many other restaurants but the parking isn't hard to find.

They have many matcha desserts and drinks to choose from but I went with the popular Matcha Latte Float (8.45) and got the full matcha soft serve on top. I also got a large size for an extra $1. It was ready in less than 5 minutes. They are generous with the soft serve but they give a lot of ice in the latte, probably because they need the soft serve to stay afloat. That part kinda sucked because there was less liquid but when the ice melts, you get more drink haha. But the soft serve was really good and full of matcha flavor. The latte was also good but on the sweeter side, which is the only thing I didn't like. I don't think you can edit the sugar levels on the matcha though but I might try next time. The price is a bit high but the soft serve portion kinda made it worth. The matcha quality also tasted good but it made me thirsty because of how much matcha they put.

They have a few tables inside so you can hang out and eat/drink with friends. I enjoyed the matcha float overall and liked the variety of matcha desserts offered. I would recommend this place for dessert when you're in this plaza.

text.text:
Got the matcha soft serve and honestly it was exactly what I expected. Not too sweet, not too much matcha. Just right.

Nice little place. Clean. There were people everywhere and all the tables were taken, but it was Sunday, so that’s kind of expected.

Staff was friendly. Cool artwork and murals inside.

I’ve been to the Little Tokyo location before and was surprised to see this one. Maybe it’s the same company, or not, honestly not sure.

There’s no restroom inside, but the plaza has public restrooms. Also free parking, which is nice if you’re driving here on purpose.

If I’m being honest, the soft serve feels a little expensive for what it is. Maybe they use some premium matcha powder But not expensive enough to stop me from buying it.

If you like matcha and you’re already in the area, definitely worth a stop.

text.text: Love this place. Best matcha soft serve. I always stop by here to get my matcha fix whenever I’m in the area

originalText.text: Love this place. Best matcha soft serve. I always stop by here to get my matcha fix whenever I’m in the area

text.text:
I went to Matcha Cafe Maiko during their grand opening week and the difference between the matcha I had then and from today’s visit (~7pm) is astounding. In my first visit, I ordered a classic iced matcha latte with oat milk. It was perfect and tasted exactly like the excellent quality from the Rowland Heights location. However, during today’s visit, and given that it was cold and raining, I wanted a hot matcha latte with oat milk. It took the employees more than 15 minutes to figure out they had no oat milk and was microwaving some other milk for the past few minutes. When they gave me a hot matcha latte with almond milk (milk substitute), the hot was certainly understated. Lukewarm at best. I had to go home to reheat it in the microwave and I unfortunately had to part ways with it and tossed the drink half way through.

TLDR: This location is best for their iced drinks and soft serves. Stay away from ordering hot.
